stall_name: Bukit Merah View Carrot Cake (Bukit Merah View Market)
reviews: Black carrot cake? Recent Google reviews for this place were really mixed...
Carrot Cake / Chye Tow Kueh (SGD $3) @ Bukit Merah View Carrot Cake. Serving for over 50 years...
Homemade Kueh Not my first time. Carrot cake is just different when not factory made...
Carrot Cake Still Made With Freshly Milled Rice!
Bukit Merah View Carrot Cake. Located at Blk 115, Bukit Merah View, Stall 01-37...
A True (Hidden) Gem Good sized chunky carrot cake fried with fish sauce and chye poh...

google.google_name: Bukit Merah View Carrot Cake 紅山景菜頭粿

reviews.author: Taste & See
reviews.text: I honestly think this is quite nice. Default option seems it's the black one. It's not strong in flavours, not too sweet or salty, but fragrant. You can taste white carrot cubes, which is of good quality. There are nice char bits. I would eat it again. $4. Only gripe is small portion.

reviews.author: Wie b l
reviews.text:
This is certainly one of the memorable Carrot Cake I've tasted, and I'm comparing to 10-12 places I've tried. This is on par with the same dish in Miao Sin Poh Piah & Carrot Cake in Balestier Food Centre. They both have their different strengths. Some people like the mushy wet style, I don't. I like the chunky, big pieces Kampong dry style. And this one comes closer to it.
The old man here is not very verbal, and he makes his own carrot cake, that leans slightly to the soft side, but not too mushy. His style is on the DRY side, and he has generous servings of Chye Poh so you will smell it.
The eggs comes out flaky, chunky and fragrant--you actually smell the egg. You also taste the carrot cake, and it leans slightly more to the salty side. And most of all, you will taste the charred on thin crispy surfaces, and the small crispy bits. And that's why it is so tasty and nice. It definitely lands in my book of being fragrant, where the egg, carrot cake, chye poh can be tasted as opposed to all homogenized and lumped together.
He uses a sweet sauce that is more watery (perhaps diluted) and that makes it wonderful, because it is not dominated by the tase of the sweet sauce. You taste and smell all the other ingredients.
The chilli was just right, not too hot and not tasteless, balanced.
I would say that definitely--in my books--taste better than the other stall in the same market, that calls themselves the "Unfogettable Carrot Cake." Maybe it's a matter of personal preference, but anytime any day, I'll go for this stall i.e. "Bukit Merah View Carrot Cake" at #01-37. Trust me, this is a good "dry" one as opposed to the wet ones.

reviews.author: LP Pua
reviews.text: I ordered a $4 plate of white carrot cake, and the portion was smaller than I had expected. The first taste was bland, lacking the cai poh flavor, and it was too oily for me. I also tried the other stall in the same hawker center, and both are of about the same standard.

reviews.author: Super Fragrance
reviews.text:
$3.50 for small white carrot cake.

Pros
Very tasty. Shiok. Big chunk here is actually good. Very savory and the chili just give the kicks. Char part gives it a more solid texture and fragrance

Cons
Some parts are really too burn. And bitter. Don't eat those! If those part was discarded by chef, will definitely be a 5 star.

Egg not crispy but then it was not meant to be for this version.

reviews.author: wiseguy Ho
reviews.text: Very inconsistent standard.  That’s a pic of white carrot cake but not much egg and a lot of burnt among the very chunky carrot cake pieces.  Shows the lack of cooking it properly

stall_name: People’s Park Bukit Merah Central (Bukit Merah Central)
reviews: Homemade beancurd with a splash of soy milk and the auntie added some Bentong ginger syrup. Silky smooth texture...

other_info.address: 163 Bukit Merah Central #02-19 Bukit Merah Central Food Centre Singapore 150163

google.google_name: 珍珠红山中心 People's Park Bukit Merah Central

reviews.author: ZH Lu
reviews.text:
Visited 28 Jan.
Dined in. Good beancurd tangyuan and service.

Previous visit:
Liang teh was served way too hot for comfort, with a clear plastic straw in a dubious translucent white plastic mug without recyclable plastic-type markings (glass should be used?) Owner should inform prior to tout-type approach and order, that Soyabean curd sold out when buying glutinous riceball.

reviews.author: fang fang
reviews.text: Really smooth and love the texture. For $1.80,its very worth it and taste much better than most others I had tried. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: CK C
reviews.text: Soya milk was not bad. Rare stall that serves the drink in a proper cup instead of disposables. E-payment available.

reviews.author: SH Choo
reviews.text: Old school soya beancurd that has been around for as long as I can remember - delicious and smooth.  So glad that my recent trip there still didn't disappoint.

reviews.author: Hedwyn Lin
reviews.text: I just passed by from a tiring day from school, so I decided to have a grass jelly drink, hoping that it would not be expensive. However, the stall owner decided to add longan into my drink, for the same price as a usual grass jelly would cost. I am very happy with her gratitude and I would come here again. I hope that this review won't let customers take advantage of the stall and always ask for discount just because she gave discount to me.

reviews: bukit merah but jurong west. is not the deep savory, dead heavy salty chup. but all braised perfect...

reviews.author: troo foo
reviews.text:
Lakeside MRT’s "Nearby" Hidden Gem – Worth the Walk (and the Queue Hiccup)

I found this kway chap stall after seeing a food blogger rave about it, saying it’s "near Lakeside MRT" – though I’d argue "nearby" is a stretch! The walk from the station is surprisingly long, but honestly? It was the perfect digestif after the heavy, hearty meal that followed.

Speaking of which, there was a little queue drama: someone cut in without a word, and even when the cook called them out, they refused to move. I gave the cook a smile and nod to say it was fine by me, and luckily, I still got my food in no time.

Taste-wise, this was right up my alley. The mixed intestines were perfectly cooked – tender and soft, nothing like the rubbery ones I’ve had at other stalls. The sauce was beautifully balanced too, with no overpowering herbal kick. I always ask for my white flat noodles without the extra white liquid, just drenched in sauce and topped with fried onions – and this time, it was absolutely heavenly. Highly recommend ordering it that way!

At $8 for a single-person set, it’s on the pricier side, but every bite made it worth the cost.

reviews.author: YB
reviews.text: Tucked away in a coffee shop that’s buried in some HDB estate 10 mins from Lakeside MRT is a surprisingly good kway chap that is easily a top 5 for me in Singapore. The kway here is differentiated by its broth that packs good flavour (albeit all the sodium hits you afterwards) while their big intestine, stomach and braised pork are all good and the chili is solid too. Portion size is a tad small for the price you pay and location but for the quality, I can’t complain too much.

reviews.author: Francis Lee
reviews.text:
This is the kind of authentic taste Kway chap I like, same like the ones I had when I was a kid.
Personally I dun like those that sell braised duck in addition to kway chup, it just taste different.

Everything is flavorful, the intestines & the pig skin are my favorite!
Pricing is quite ok, $4.40 for the basic set with big Kway.
Who says west side no nice food, we the west just quietly enjoy our gems. 😂🤪 Peace peace just kidding.

reviews.author: AP.
reviews.text: Really tasty and good! If you have the stomach for more, grab the big bow of kway, it’s so silky smooth! Will definitely recommend and be back if I’m in the around.

reviews.author: JiSheng Js
reviews.text:
The queue moves fast, and the food is worth the wait. The braised meats and sides were well-marinated, tender, and full of flavor. The soup was rich, and delicious, pairing perfectly with the silky kway.

A great spot for kway chap lovers, and this branch maintains the same quality as their original. Highly recommended if you’re in the area!

stall_name: Chai Chuan Tou Yang Rou Tang (Bukit Merah View Market)
reviews: Comforting Mutton Soup. $6 bowl of Mixed Mutton soup, filled with (boneless) mutton ribs and stomach...
Mixed Mutton Soup. There’s only a few stalls here that always had a long queue in front...
Stomach and soft meat 6nett. The queue is annoying cos its 30mins thereabouts...
Mutton soup. A good bowl of mutton soup!, $7 for a generous portion.
Mixed Beef/Mutton Soup ($7). Came here at 11.30am, and there was already a queue of 8 people...

other_info.address: 115 Bukit Merah View #01-51 Bukit Merah View Market & Hawker Centre Singapore 151115

google.google_name: Chai Chuan Tou Yang Rou Tang

reviews.author: LexCinCout
Jennifer C
reviews.text:
Stall is only open for 1-2 hours. Most of the time sold out in 1 hour or 1.5hours. Queue is mad long but moves pretty fast.

Probably the best mutton soup I’ve had. No strong smell of mutton, meat is super soft and soup is very flavorful. Must try! Please come the moment they open for business!

reviews.author: 葉
reviews.text: An unmissable queue snakes towards this stall at peak lunch hours, with neighbourhood regulars and office crowds waiting to fill their stomachs with bowls of full-bodied, herbaceous Chinese mutton soup. Served with rice and topped with finely chopped fresh ginger and coriander, the tender lamb meat and innards are balanced with a hints of freshness with each bite and mouthful of sweet, flavourful broth.

reviews.author: Lawrence Leong
reviews.text: Stall is run by mother and daughter both whom are very nice and friendly. Love the mutton soup as it is with strong herbal taste. Ordered 2 bowls at $7 and $10. Quantity of meat is generous and soup is refillable. Easily one of the most satisfying mutton soup I have tried.

reviews.author: BoonChuen Low
reviews.text: Reached there around 12:20 pm and there were already about 20 people queuing. I joined the queue and received my order in about 20 minutes. I ordered the $8 mixed parts and they were generous in the ingredients. The soup is nice and with herbal taste. I will definitely eat there again.

stall_name: 786 Char Kway Teow (Bukit Merah View Market)
reviews: Halal Char Kway Teow. No pork no lard...
Halal Char Kway Teow anyone? Although it has no pork no lard, its still quite...
A Hidden Halal Char Kway Teow in Bukit Merah!

other_info.address: 115 Bukit Merah View #01-28 Bukit Merah View Market & Hawker Centre Singapore 151115

reviews.author: Alice Wong
reviews.text: The char kuey teow tasted old school like, quite nice. Cockles were fresh. Note: it is a Halal stall, so no lap cheong, etc.

reviews.author: Super Fragrance
reviews.text:
Ordered small ckt for $4.50

Pros
Taste. Sweet but I guess more important spicy which brought out the savoriness.

Many fresh hum. At least relatively speaking. It is not a big plate but quite a few hum. Bloodiness adds to the taste.

Eggs is also very distinctive cooked.

Cons
Well it is meant to be halal. Not halal certified but owner is Muslim. So no lup cheong or pork lard to go with it. Sadly.

Still a delicious plate

reviews.author: Sri Rahayu
reviews.text: I ordered the Halal Char Kway Teow for $5.50 with an extra cockles  for $2. The taste was authentic, not overly oily, with a nice wokhei aroma. The addition of  chili padi  gave it a moderate level of spiciness. Unfortunately, there was no halal lap cheong to complete the authentic flavor.

reviews.author: Ben
reviews.text: Got the $4.50 plate, which was 75% filling so most people should be satisfied with the $5.50 one. The chilli is pretty moderate but if you don't eat spicy at all it may be a little too much for you to handle. Flavours are great with the mix of sweet and spiciness that blends well together. Also didn't realise the stall was Muslim owned until writing the review, a nice bonus I guess.

reviews.author: Tan
reviews.text:
✅️ Halal
✅️ QR Code payment
✅️ Delicious
✅️ Easy to order
✅️ Friendly staff
✅️ Not oily

❌️ No pork lard

Do note that as this is a halal-certified store, they do not have pork lard. Surprisingly good though! I love how it doesn't feel oily and still has the char kway teow taste. It's very nicely done! Be patient with the stallholder as he is doing everything by himself. It is worth the wait, can afford to get the medium sized if you have an appetite.
